{"tests":[{"filename":"001_basic_file_copy.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dst.txt"
printf 'hello world\n' > "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if cmp -s "$src" "$dst"; then exit 0; fi
echo "destination content mismatch" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"basic SOURCE DEST file copy","expected":"destination file has same contents as source","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"002_multiple_sources_directory.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
a="$tmpdir/a.txt"
b="$tmpdir/b.txt"
out="$tmpdir/out"
printf 'a\n' > "$a"
printf 'b\n' > "$b"
mkdir "$out"
if ! "$UTIL" "$a" "$b" "$out"; then echo "cp failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
count=$(find "$out" -maxdepth 1 -type f | wc -l)
if [[ "$count" -eq 2 ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "expected two files in target directory" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"SOURCE... DIRECTORY form","expected":"target directory contains two copied files","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"003_target_directory_t.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
out="$tmpdir/target"
printf 'via -t\n' > "$src"
mkdir "$out"
if ! "$UTIL" -t "$out" "$src"; then echo "cp -t failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if cmp -s "$src" "$out/source.txt"; then exit 0; fi
echo "-t destination content mismatch" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-t/--target-directory","expected":"source is copied under target directory","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"004_no_target_directory_T_error.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/existing_dir"
printf 'data\n' > "$src"
mkdir "$dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" -T "$src" "$dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
if [[ "$status" -ne 0 ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "cp -T unexpectedly succeeded for existing directory" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-T/--no-target-directory error with existing directory DEST","expected":"utility exits nonzero","expected_to_fail":true},{"filename":"005_recursive_R_directory.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
srcdir="$tmpdir/srcdir"
dstdir="$tmpdir/dstdir"
mkdir -p "$srcdir/sub"
printf 'nested\n' > "$srcdir/sub/file.txt"
if ! "$UTIL" -R "$srcdir" "$dstdir"; then echo "cp -R failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dstdir/sub/file.txt")" == "nested" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "recursive copy missing nested content" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-R/-r/--recursive directory copy","expected":"nested file is present in copied directory","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"006_archive_preserves_symlink.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
srcdir="$tmpdir/srcdir"
dstdir="$tmpdir/dstdir"
mkdir "$srcdir"
printf 'target\n' > "$srcdir/file.txt"
ln -s "file.txt" "$srcdir/link.txt"
if ! "$UTIL" -a "$srcdir" "$dstdir"; then echo "cp -a failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ -L "$dstdir/link.txt" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "archive copy did not preserve symlink" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-a/--archive includes -dR symlink preservation","expected":"copied link remains a symbolic link","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"007_no_dereference_P_symlink.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
target="$tmpdir/target.txt"
link="$tmpdir/link.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/copied_link.txt"
printf 'referent\n' > "$target"
ln -s "$target" "$link"
if ! "$UTIL" -P "$link" "$dst"; then echo "cp -P failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ -L "$dst" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-P did not copy symlink as symlink" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-P/--no-dereference","expected":"destination is a symbolic link","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"008_dereference_L_symlink.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
target="$tmpdir/target.txt"
link="$tmpdir/link.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/copied.txt"
printf 'referent\n' > "$target"
ln -s "$target" "$link"
if ! "$UTIL" -L "$link" "$dst"; then echo "cp -L failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ ! -L "$dst" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-L copied the symlink instead of its referent" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-L/--dereference","expected":"destination is not a symbolic link","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"009_command_line_H_symlink.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
realdir="$tmpdir/real"
linkdir="$tmpdir/linkdir"
dst="$tmpdir/copy"
mkdir "$realdir"
printf 'inside\n' > "$realdir/file.txt"
ln -s "$realdir" "$linkdir"
if ! "$UTIL" -RH "$linkdir" "$dst"; then echo "cp -RH failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst/file.txt")" == "inside" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-H did not follow command-line directory symlink" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-H follows command-line symbolic links in SOURCE","expected":"copied tree contains referent directory file","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"010_symbolic_link_s.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/link_to_source"
printf 'data\n' > "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" -s "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp -s failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(readlink "$dst")" == "$src" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "symbolic link target mismatch" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-s/--symbolic-link","expected":"destination symlink points to source path","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"011_hard_link_l.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/hardlink.txt"
printf 'data\n' > "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" -l "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp -l failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(stat -c '%d:%i' "$src")" == "$(stat -c '%d:%i' "$dst")" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "destination is not a hard link to source" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-l/--link","expected":"source and destination have the same device and inode","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"012_preserve_p_mode.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'mode\n' > "$src"
chmod 640 "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" -p "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp -p failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(stat -c '%a' "$dst")" == "640" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-p did not preserve mode" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-p preserves mode, ownership, timestamps","expected":"destination mode is preserved as 640","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"013_preserve_timestamps.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'time\n' > "$src"
touch -d '2001-02-03 04:05:06 UTC' "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" --preserve=timestamps "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp --preserve=timestamps failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(stat -c '%Y' "$dst")" == "$(stat -c '%Y' "$src")" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "timestamp was not preserved" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--preserve=timestamps","expected":"destination mtime equals source mtime","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"014_no_preserve_timestamps.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'time\n' > "$src"
touch -d '2001-02-03 04:05:06 UTC' "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" --no-preserve=timestamps "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp --no-preserve=timestamps failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(stat -c '%Y' "$dst")" != "$(stat -c '%Y' "$src")" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "timestamp was unexpectedly preserved" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--no-preserve=timestamps","expected":"destination mtime differs from old source mtime","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"015_attributes_only.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'nonempty data\n' > "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" --attributes-only "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp --attributes-only failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(stat -c '%s' "$dst")" -eq 0 ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "attributes-only copy copied file data" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--attributes-only","expected":"destination file has zero size","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"016_parents_full_source_name.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/parent/child/file.txt"
out="$tmpdir/out"
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$src")" "$out"
printf 'parents\n' > "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" --parents "$src" "$out"; then echo "cp --parents failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
expected_path="$out$src"
if [[ "$(cat "$expected_path")" == "parents" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "--parents did not create full source path under directory" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--parents","expected":"full source pathname is created under target directory","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"017_backup_with_suffix.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
if ! "$UTIL" --backup=simple --suffix=.bak "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp --backup --suffix failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst.bak")" == "old" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "backup with custom suffix does not contain old destination" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--backup[=CONTROL] and -S/--suffix","expected":"custom-suffix backup contains old destination data","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"018_short_b_backup_suffix_env.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
if ! SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX=.save "$UTIL" -b "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp -b failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst.save")" == "old" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-b backup with SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX missing old data" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-b backup and SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX","expected":"environment-suffix backup contains old destination data","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"019_force_backup_same_file.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/same.txt"
printf 'same-file\n' > "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" -f --backup=simple --suffix=.bak "$src" "$src"; then echo "cp -f --backup same file failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$src.bak")" == "same-file" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "same-file force backup not created with original data" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"special case: -f with --backup when SOURCE and DEST are same file","expected":"backup of source is created","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"020_no_clobber_n.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
if ! "$UTIL" -n "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp -n failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst")" == "old" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-n overwrote existing destination" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-n/--no-clobber","expected":"existing destination content is unchanged","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"021_update_none.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
if ! "$UTIL" --update=none "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp --update=none failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst")" == "old" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "--update=none overwrote existing destination" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--update=none","expected":"existing destination is skipped without replacement","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"022_update_older_replaces.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
touch -d '2020-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$dst"
touch -d '2021-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$src"
if ! "$UTIL" --update=older "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp --update=older failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst")" == "new" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "--update=older did not replace older destination" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--update=older","expected":"older destination is replaced by newer source","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"023_u_skips_newer_destination.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'old-source\n' > "$src"
printf 'new-dest\n' > "$dst"
touch -d '2020-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$src"
touch -d '2021-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$dst"
if ! "$UTIL" -u "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp -u failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst")" == "new-dest" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-u replaced a newer destination" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-u equivalent to --update=older","expected":"newer destination is not replaced","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"024_interactive_i_decline.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
if ! printf 'n\n' | "$UTIL" -i "$src" "$dst" >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "cp -i failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst")" == "old" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "-i overwrote destination after negative response" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"-i/--interactive prompt before overwrite","expected":"negative response leaves destination unchanged","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"025_remove_destination_breaks_link.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
peer="$tmpdir/peer.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
ln "$dst" "$peer"
if ! "$UTIL" --remove-destination "$src" "$dst"; then echo "cp --remove-destination failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(stat -c '%d:%i' "$dst")" != "$(stat -c '%d:%i' "$peer")" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "destination was not removed before replacement" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--remove-destination","expected":"replaced destination no longer shares inode with old hard link","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"026_strip_trailing_slashes.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
target="$tmpdir/target.txt"
link="$tmpdir/link.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'stripped\n' > "$target"
ln -s "$target" "$link"
if ! "$UTIL" --strip-trailing-slashes "$link/" "$dst"; then echo "cp --strip-trailing-slashes failed" >&2; exit 1; fi
if [[ "$(cat "$dst")" == "stripped" ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "trailing slash was not stripped from source" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--strip-trailing-slashes","expected":"symlink source with trailing slash is copied after slash removal","expected_to_fail":false},{"filename":"027_nonexistent_source_error.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
missing="$tmpdir/missing.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
set +e
"$UTIL" "$missing" "$dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
if [[ "$status" -ne 0 ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "cp unexpectedly succeeded for nonexistent source" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"documented SOURCE must exist to be copied","expected":"utility exits nonzero for nonexistent source","expected_to_fail":true},{"filename":"028_update_none_fail_error.sh","body":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/source.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dest.txt"
printf 'new\n' > "$src"
printf 'old\n' > "$dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --update=none-fail "$src" "$dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
if [[ "$status" -ne 0 ]]; then exit 0; fi
echo "--update=none-fail did not fail on skipped existing destination" >&2
exit 1
","exercises":"--update=none-fail error on skipped existing destination","expected":"utility exits nonzero","expected_to_fail":true}]}
